---
title: "Section C"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-15"
---

#Section C - Full Stack Design

#Overview

The goal is to build a simple full-stack application to analyze mortality rates across facilities, time, and locations.

Due to time constraints, this submission focuses on system design and architecture rather than full implementation.


# System Architecture

Frontend:
- React (or simple HTML + JavaScript)
- Responsible for user interaction and visualization

Backend:
- Python (Flask / FastAPI)
- Handles filtering, aggregation, and API responses

Database:
- CSV or SQLite
- Stores mortality dataset

# Backend Design

# 1. Filtering

Supports:
- Year
- Month
- State
- ZIP Code
- Facility Name


# 2. API Endpoints

# Summary Endpoint

GET/Summary
Returns:

- total facilities
- average mortality rate
- min / max mortality
- top 10 highest
- top 10 lowest
# Table Endpoint

GET/tablepage=1&pageSize=10
Returns:

- paginated data
- total count
# Analysis Endpoint

GET/Analysis
Returns:

- monthly trend
- state comparison
- zip comparison
- distribution
# Frontend Design
# Page 1: Summary

Components:
- Filters panel
- KPI cards (total, avg, min, max)
- Top 10 lists
- Data table (paginated)

# Page 2: Analysis

Charts:
- Line chart (monthly trend)
- Bar chart (state comparison)
- Histogram (distribution)

# Data Handling

- Parse date into Year and Month
- Handle missing values (drop or fill)
- Sort values for ranking
- Ensure consistent data types

# Optional Improvements

- Export results as CSV
- Compare multiple states
- Highlight outliers
- Add caching for performance

# Conclusion

This design focuses on simplicity, clarity, and scalability. The backend handles all computation, while the frontend focuses on visualization and user interaction.
